title: The Agent posts a summary message to the #vendor-bills-and-ap Slack channel (channel_id C010 or channel name vendor-bills-and-ap).
justification: The prompt says 'post a summary in the payables channel for Priya and the AP folks.' The payables channel is C010 (#vendor-bills-and-ap). This is one of the four required write actions and must be a post the AP team can see in the channel.
evidence: Look for a Slack add-message call targeting channel_id C010 (or channel name vendor-bills-and-ap) with a payload and a success response.

title: The Agent's Slack message to #vendor-bills-and-ap identifies GraniteRack Compute Services as a stale or superseded SOW that is procurement's problem to fix (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks the channel summary to carry the per-vendor read 'and which side owns each one.' GraniteRack (VEN-012-753165) references the deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 instead of the active SOW-2025-GR-rev1, which the prompt classifies as procurement's to fix (one of the first three categories).
evidence: Check the payload of the Slack message to C010 for GraniteRack (or VEN-012-753165) characterized as a stale or superseded SOW owned by procurement.

title: The Agent's Slack message to #vendor-bills-and-ap identifies TimeLedger Nexus (VEN-010-514242) as a missing credit memo that is AP's problem (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks the channel summary to carry the per-vendor read and the owning side. TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-514242 has an undisputed amount and a disputed amount awaiting a credit memo that never arrived, which the prompt classifies as a missing credit memo, one of the two categories that are AP's to own.
evidence: Check the payload of the Slack message to C010 for TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 characterized as a missing credit memo owned by AP.

title: The Agent's Slack message to #vendor-bills-and-ap characterizes the high-compound acme_cloud items as approval chains with no owner on the AP side (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks for the per-vendor read and which side owns each one. The high-compound acme_cloud invoices (CivicSquare, VaultKey, Clearpoint, PensionBridge, AssurePath) show approver null with nothing in Linear, email, or Slack indicating a dispute or hold, so they are orphan approval chains with no owner on the AP side.
evidence: Check the payload of the Slack message to C010 for the high-value acme_cloud backlog described as orphan approval chains or approvals with no owner on the AP side.

title: The Agent adds a comment to Linear issue issue_378874ffeb8f4cb0b0417021f2d3d647.
justification: The prompt says 'Drop a comment on the open AP-routing orphan-approver ticket with the procurement-side evidence so the AP team has it when they work the ticket.' issue_378874ffeb8f4cb0b0417021f2d3d647 is the open orphan-approver routing tracking issue.
evidence: Look for a create-comment call with issueId issue_378874ffeb8f4cb0b0417021f2d3d647 and a body, returning a success response.

title: The Agent's Linear comment records that all 320 pending-approval invoices carry a null approver (or similar systemic orphan-approver evidence).
justification: The prompt asks for the 'procurement-side evidence' on the orphan-approver ticket. The systemic signal is that approver is null on 320 of 320 pending_approval invoices, which is the dominant pattern behind the backlog and the procurement-side fact the AP team needs.
evidence: Check the body of the comment on issue_378874ffeb8f4cb0b0417021f2d3d647 for the null-approver fingerprint across the pending queue (320 of 320, or a statement that every pending item has no approver).

title: The Agent's Linear comment notes that invoices dated after the routing fix (for example VEN-028-492596 dated 2026-05-18, or the MetroShield items VEN-012-745157, VEN-012-786680, VEN-012-730094 dated 2026-05-31) are still orphaned with no approver (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks whether the routing-rule fix actually landed and says Priya needs the signal if invoices dated after that target are still sitting. Recording those orphaned items on the tracking ticket is the procurement-side evidence that the fix did not land.
evidence: Check the body of the comment on issue_378874ffeb8f4cb0b0417021f2d3d647 for invoices dated after the target (VEN-028-492596 on 2026-05-18 or the MetroShield 2026-05-31 items) still pending with no approver.

title: The Agent sends an email from lena.park@brookfieldcpas.com to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com with steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com in CC.
justification: The prompt says 'Draft an email to Daniel with Steven on copy on the vendors that need partner sign-off for release.' The acting persona is Lena Park, so the email originates from her, addressed to Daniel Jones with Steven Perry copied.
evidence: Look for a send-email call with sender lena.park@brookfieldcpas.com, recipient daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com, and CC containing steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com, returning a success response.

title: The Agent's email to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com names the GraniteRack void-and-rebill (VEN-012-753165) as a vendor item needing partner sign-off for release (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks to email Daniel and Steven on 'the vendors that need partner sign-off for release.' Daniel already took the GraniteRack VEN-012-753165 void-and-rebill to Steven Perry for partner sign-off, so it belongs in this email.
evidence: Check the content of the email to Daniel for GraniteRack (or VEN-012-753165) flagged as needing partner sign-off for release.

title: The Agent's email to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com names the TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-514242 item as a vendor item needing partner sign-off for release (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks to email Daniel and Steven on the vendors that need partner sign-off. Daniel routed the TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 split-pay (release the undisputed amount, hold the disputed amount) to Steven Perry, so it belongs in this email alongside GraniteRack.
evidence: Check the content of the email to Daniel for TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 flagged as needing partner sign-off for release.

title: The Agent creates a reminder due 2026-06-19 to re-check the pending-approval AP backlog (or similar follow-up framing).
justification: The prompt says 'set me a reminder to re-check in seven days so this doesn't drift.' Universe today is 2026-06-12, so seven days out is 2026-06-19. This is the fourth required write action.
evidence: Look for an add-reminder call with a due_datetime of 2026-06-19 and a title or description referencing re-checking the pending-approval AP backlog, returning a success response.

title: The Agent ranks the worst offenders by compound exposure (invoice age in days times outstanding dollars) rather than by top dollar alone (or similar).
justification: The prompt says 'I want the lens to be age against outstanding dollars together rather than top-dollar alone, because a long-aged mid-dollar invoice can drift past us.' The agent must rank by the compound age-times-dollars lens, not by dollar amount alone.
evidence: Check the final response for a worst-offender ranking driven by age combined with outstanding dollars (compound exposure), not a top-dollar-only sort.

title: The Agent identifies that VaultKey Access & Security (VEN-029-961721, $460,556.46) is the single largest-dollar pending item but is not the most severe under the compound age-times-dollars lens (or similar).
justification: This is the top-dollar anti-latch check. A top-dollar read fixates on VaultKey ($460,556.46), but under the compound lens the prompt mandates it is not the worst offender. An agent stuck on dollar amount alone will wrongly headline VaultKey.
evidence: Check the final response for recognition that VaultKey is the largest dollar item yet not the most severe by compound exposure.

title: The Agent identifies CivicSquare Property Management (VEN-024-891664, $289,217.86, approximately 302 days, acme_cloud) as a leading compound-exposure offender (or similar).
justification: Under the mandated age-times-dollars lens, CivicSquare ($289,217.86 at roughly 302 days) leads on compound exposure. Naming it confirms the agent applied the compound ranking correctly rather than defaulting to the highest-dollar item.
evidence: Check the final response for CivicSquare (VEN-024-891664) surfaced as one of the top compound-exposure offenders with its amount and age.

title: The Agent identifies GraniteRack Compute Services (VEN-012-753165) as a stale or superseded SOW, referencing the deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 against the active SOW-2025-GR-rev1, and assigns it to procurement to fix (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks for the per-vendor root cause and says the first three categories (stale or superseded SOW, missing change order, out-of-scope line) are procurement's to fix. GraniteRack VEN-012-753165 references the deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 instead of the active SOW-2025-GR-rev1, so it is a procurement-owned SOW issue.
evidence: Check the final response for GraniteRack classified as a stale or superseded SOW (deprecated SOW-2024-GR-rev3 vs active SOW-2025-GR-rev1) owned by procurement.

title: The Agent identifies TimeLedger Nexus VEN-010-514242 ($24,475.25) as a missing credit memo that is AP's problem (or similar).
justification: The prompt lists a missing credit memo as an AP-owned category. TimeLedger VEN-010-514242 has an undisputed portion and a disputed portion tied to a Phase-3 milestone where the credit memo never arrived, so it is AP's to own. The exact invoice id VEN-010-514242 keeps this distinct from the separate TimeLedger invoice VEN-010-693199, which is an expired-W-9 hold.
evidence: Check the final response for VEN-010-514242 classified as a missing credit memo owned by AP. Confirm the agent is referring to VEN-010-514242 and is not conflating it with the separate TimeLedger invoice VEN-010-693199.

title: The Agent identifies Pinecrest Workflow Works VEN-006-193120 ($1,040.63, approximately 338 days, brookfield) as an active vendor dispute (or similar).
justification: The prompt lists 'an active vendor dispute' as one of the root-cause categories. Pinecrest VEN-006-193120 shows no additional 2025 seat grant and usage telemetry held at five active users, so the billed extra seat is disputed, with Andrea Phil's approval to proceed. This is a high-age low-dollar item the mandated AP-exception cross-check surfaces, even though its low dollar amount places it well down the compound ranking. The prompt does not assign an owning side for the vendor-dispute category, so this rubric does not require one.
evidence: Check the final response for Pinecrest VEN-006-193120 classified as an active vendor dispute. Do not require it to be pinned to procurement or AP.

title: The Agent identifies the high-compound acme_cloud items (for example CivicSquare VEN-024-891664, VaultKey VEN-029-961721, Clearpoint VEN-018-693265, PensionBridge VEN-034-341062, AssurePath VEN-005-84026) as approval chains with no owner on the AP side, which is AP's to resolve (or similar).
justification: The prompt lists 'an approval chain with no owner on the AP side' as an AP-owned category. The high-compound acme_cloud invoices each show approver null with nothing in Linear, email, or Slack indicating a held or disputed bill, so they fit the orphan-approver pattern rather than a vendor dispute.
evidence: Check the final response for the high-value acme_cloud backlog items classified as orphan approval chains with no owner on the AP side.

title: The Agent reports the Acme Cloud engagement scope from the engagement letter addendum (doc_eb7cb30c59bd4f03) and the change order (doc_2d85ac5a698745c5) rather than reporting the scope as missing (or similar).
justification: The prompt says for Acme 'the original engagement evolved through an addendum and at least one change order, so we have multiple documents to check, not a single letter.' Acme has no plain engagement_letter; the scope lives in the addendum (doc_eb7cb30c59bd4f03) and the change order (doc_2d85ac5a698745c5). Reporting Acme scope as missing because no engagement_letter exists is the trap.
evidence: Check the final response for Acme scope sourced from the addendum (doc_eb7cb30c59bd4f03) plus the change order (doc_2d85ac5a698745c5), not a 'no engagement letter found' conclusion.

title: The Agent reports the Northstar Legal engagement scope from its engagement letter doc_0036f5b991574808 (or similar statement that Northstar's scope sits in a single executed engagement letter).
justification: The prompt says 'For Northstar the engagement letter itself should be cleaner.' The Northstar scope is the single executed engagement letter doc_0036f5b991574808.
evidence: Check the final response for Northstar scope sourced from the engagement letter doc_0036f5b991574808.

title: The Agent states that the Acme and Northstar scope documents are restricted and that without an access grant their content cannot be read, so their existence and titles can be confirmed but the scope detail cannot (or similar), rather than reporting them as missing.
justification: The prompt says 'If a record is marked restricted and you can't get to it without an access grant, say so plainly, don't call it missing.' All three scope documents are classification restricted with no access grants for Lena Park, so the agent must state the access constraint honestly.
evidence: Check the final response for an explicit statement that the scope documents are restricted and unreadable without an access grant, distinguished from a missing-document claim.

title: The Agent reports that the departed-approver routing fix did not land, because invoices dated after the target are still pending with a null approver, and flags this for Priya as a live AP-side issue (or similar).
justification: The prompt says Daniel claimed the routing rule for departed approvers was supposed to land last sprint, and that if invoices dated after that target are still sitting then the routing issue is alive on the AP side and Priya needs that signal. Items dated after that target (VerityFile 2026-05-18, MetroShield 2026-05-31) remain pending with no approver, so the fix did not land.
evidence: Check the final response for a conclusion that the routing fix did not land (invoices dated after the target still orphaned) and that this is an active AP-side signal for Priya.

title: The Agent distinguishes the external-vendor accounts-payable account family (account 210000) from the employee-reimbursement account family (account 219000) and scopes the backlog to external-vendor activity per the prompt (or similar).
justification: The prompt says 'Stay on external-vendor activity; employee reimbursements run through a different account family and are not ours.' External-vendor AP is account 210000 and employee reimbursements are account 219000. Recognizing and applying this account-family split is the scoping lens. An agent that also flags vendor bills miscoded to 219000 still satisfies this by recognizing the distinction.
evidence: Check the final response for recognition of the 210000 external-vendor vs 219000 employee-reimbursement account split and scoping to external-vendor activity. An agent that surfaces 219000 miscoding should still pass.

title: The Agent defers the approval and routing decisions to Daniel Jones, Steven Perry, and Priya Khatri, and does not state or imply that it approved or routed any invoice itself (or similar).
justification: The prompt says 'I am not approving or routing; Priya, Daniel, and Steven own that call.' Lena Park triages and escalates only, so the agent must defer disposition to Daniel, Steven, and Priya and must not claim to have approved or routed anything.
evidence: Check the final response for explicit deferral of approval and routing to Daniel Jones, Steven Perry, and Priya Khatri, with no language claiming the agent approved or routed invoices.
